description: subprocess.run(command, shell=True) passes LLM-generated command string directly to shell; same pattern present in all 13 agent files (s01-s12, s_full) and 2 reference scripts
suggested_fix: Document trust boundary (local use only) and remove false-security blocklist, or replace shell=True with allowlist-based command dispatch

description: Blocklist ['rm -rf /', 'sudo', 'shutdown', 'reboot', '> /dev/'] is trivially bypassed by trailing space, uppercase variants, or indirect paths; present in all agent run_bash() implementations
suggested_fix: Remove the blocklist (honest about capability) or replace with a proper allowlist of permitted command prefixes; document that these agents require a trusted user environment

description: anthropic>=0.25.0, python-dotenv>=1.0.0, pyyaml>=6.0 allow unbounded major-version upgrades on each fresh pip install, creating supply-chain and breaking-change risk
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions (==) or add upper-bound constraints, e.g. anthropic>=0.25.0,<2.0.0

description: All npm dependencies use ^ ranges; minor/patch updates can introduce regressions in a deployed Next.js app
suggested_fix: Pin to exact versions for the web app's lock file; caret ranges are acceptable for dev-only tooling

description: Skill provides agent design guidance but declares no response template or structure for how the LLM should format its output when the skill is active
suggested_fix: Add a brief '## Output Format' section with a suggested response structure (e.g. design summary, capability list, anti-patterns flagged)

description: 'Load it when relevant' does not specify what conditions make knowledge relevant; reader cannot act on this without criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with measurable trigger: e.g. 'Load it when the user asks about [specific domain]'

description: 'Perform thorough code reviews' in the description does not define what thoroughness requires; checklist body provides criteria but the descriptor is still undefined
suggested_fix: Replace 'thorough' with the specific scope: 'security, correctness, performance, and maintainability'

description: 'conducting comprehensive code reviews' provides no specifics; the subsequent checklist defines scope but the framing word is still vague
suggested_fix: Drop the adjective or replace with enumerated scope matching the checklist sections

description: 'Meaningful, specific checks' uses 'Meaningful' without defining what separates a meaningful assertion from a trivial one
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete guidance: e.g. 'Assertions must verify observable behavior, not implementation details'

description: MCP builder skill provides templates and best practices but no guidance on how the LLM should structure its response when helping a user build an MCP server
suggested_fix: Add '## Response Format' section: confirm tool list, show project structure summary, highlight security checklist results

description: 'Return meaningful error messages' does not define what makes an error message meaningful (include error code? user action? context?)
suggested_fix: Specify: 'Error messages must include the tool name, the input that failed validation, and the expected format'

description: 'Never expose sensitive operations without auth' does not define which operations are sensitive; readers cannot apply this without a criteria list
suggested_fix: Enumerate: e.g. 'operations that write files, execute commands, or access credentials require authentication'

description: 'Tools should be safe to retry' does not define what safety means in this context (idempotent? no side effects? limited blast radius?)
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'Tools must be idempotent — calling the same tool twice with the same arguments must produce the same result'

description: PDF skill teaches workflows and libraries but provides no template for how the LLM should structure its reply when applying the skill
suggested_fix: Add a brief '## Response Format' noting which tool was selected, the command run, and the output summary

description: '(preferred)' labels Option 1 as preferred without stating why; reader cannot make an informed choice in edge cases
suggested_fix: Specify the preference criterion: e.g. '(preferred when poppler-utils is available — fastest, no Python dependency)'

description: '(recommended)' for Markdown-to-PDF conversion is stated without criteria; reader cannot evaluate when to deviate
suggested_fix: Specify: e.g. '(recommended when source is already Markdown — avoids layout issues from programmatic PDF generation)'

description: 'PDFs may contain various character encodings' — 'various' is a placeholder; adds no actionable information
suggested_fix: Replace with: 'PDFs may use Latin-1, UTF-8, or proprietary CID-keyed fonts; use errors="replace" when decoding extracted text'
